test_plan.strategy: Validate the upgraded Phaser-based embeddable game through deterministic rule tests, browser integration checks, responsive embed-layout validation, and end-to-end player-flow verification against the new product baseline.

test_cases.title: Local operator reaches first playable state with documented flow
preconditions: Node.js 18+ and npm 10+ are installed
Dependencies have not yet been installed in the repository
test_cases.given: A clean checkout of the repository root is available on a standard desktop machine with Node.js 18+ and npm 10+
test_cases.when: The operator runs `npm install` and `npm run dev`, then opens `http://127.0.0.1:5173/` in a desktop browser
test_cases.then: The first playable state is reachable in 5 minutes or less from the repository root and the start overlay is visible
steps: Install dependencies from the repository root
Start the local Vite dev server
Open the documented localhost URL
Confirm the title, objective text, and Start button are visible
test_cases.expected_result: A local operator can reach the first playable state with the documented setup flow in 5 minutes or less from the repository root on a standard Node.js environment.

test_cases.title: Embedded container renders correctly at 800x450
preconditions: A host page can render the game in an iframe or bounded container
test_cases.given: The game is mounted inside an 800x450 container
test_cases.when: The start overlay and first gameplay frame are rendered in the container
test_cases.then: The main playfield is not clipped and primary controls remain visible inside the 800x450 container
steps: Open the game inside a fixed 800x450 host container
Inspect the title overlay and active gameplay frame
Verify that the main lane, HUD, and primary Start action are all visible
test_cases.expected_result: In a standard 800x450 embedded container, the game renders without clipping the main playfield or hiding primary controls.

test_cases.title: Player can start a run within 30 seconds using only visible instructions
preconditions: First-time player has no prior explanation from an operator
test_cases.given: A first-time player opens the game and sees only the visible title, objective, and controls on the start overlay
test_cases.when: The player attempts to begin a run without outside help
test_cases.then: The player starts an active run within 30 seconds of the initial load using only visible on-screen instructions
steps: Load the start screen
Remove any external written instructions or spoken explanation
Observe whether the player can identify and use the Start action
test_cases.expected_result: A first-time player can start an active run within 30 seconds of the initial load using only visible on-screen instructions.

test_cases.title: Crosshair remains visible and gameplay-critical targets are readable
preconditions: An active gameplay session is running with at least one civilian and one zombie visible
test_cases.given: A first-time observer watches an active run with civilians, zombies, bunker, and HUD visible
test_cases.when: The observer is given 1 second to identify the crosshair and distinguish threat vs protected target
test_cases.then: The crosshair remains visible throughout active gameplay and primary targets can be distinguished in less than 1 second by a first-time observer
steps: Start a run and wait until civilians and zombies are simultaneously visible
Confirm the custom crosshair is visible
Time whether a first-time observer can identify civilian vs zombie in under 1 second
test_cases.expected_result: The custom crosshair remains visible throughout active gameplay and primary targets can be distinguished in less than 1 second by a first-time observer.

test_cases.title: Mouse aim and left-click shot work in every active gameplay state
preconditions: Game session is in active play and not paused, completed, or game over
test_cases.given: The player is in an active gameplay state with an unlocked browser pointer
test_cases.when: The player moves the mouse and presses left click
test_cases.then: The player can aim with the mouse and fire with left click in every active gameplay state without additional tutorial steps
steps: Start a run
Move the pointer across the playable area
Press left click while the run is active
Verify the shot fires and the crosshair moves as expected
test_cases.expected_result: The player can aim with the mouse and fire with left click in every active gameplay state without additional tutorial steps.

test_cases.title: Bunker is recognized as safe endpoint within 30 seconds
preconditions: First-time player has not previously seen the bunker asset
test_cases.given: A first-time player starts the first run and sees civilians moving toward the right-side bunker
test_cases.when: The player is asked where civilians are trying to go within the first 30 seconds of the run
test_cases.then: The player identifies the bunker as the civilian destination and safe endpoint within 30 seconds of entering the first run
steps: Start a run
Let the player observe the scene without explanation
Ask the player to identify the civilians' goal
test_cases.expected_result: A first-time player can identify the bunker as the civilian destination and safe endpoint within 30 seconds of entering the first run.

test_cases.title: Friendly fire applies exact score and life penalty immediately
preconditions: A civilian is visible and targetable in active gameplay
Current score is 100 and current life is 100
test_cases.given: The run is active, score is 100, life is 100, and one civilian is under the crosshair
test_cases.when: The player fires one left-click shot that hits the civilian
test_cases.then: Score becomes 85, life becomes 90, and negative visual and audio feedback occur in the same interaction or next rendered frame
steps: Set score to 100 and life to 100 in a deterministic test state
Place one civilian under the crosshair
Fire one shot
Assert score, life, and feedback outputs
test_cases.expected_result: A player-shot civilian immediately applies -15 score and -10 life with negative visual and audio feedback in the same interaction or next rendered frame.

test_cases.title: Zombie contact damage by category uses exact values
preconditions: Rule helper can apply civilian-loss penalties for each zombie category
test_cases.given: A session state starts at life 100 and civiliansLost 0
test_cases.when: Normal, elite/alpha, and brute contact rules are applied separately
test_cases.then: Normal contact leaves life 90 and civiliansLost 1, elite/alpha contact leaves life 85 and civiliansLost 1, and brute contact leaves life 80 and civiliansLost 1
steps: Create isolated session state with life 100 and civiliansLost 0
Apply normal zombie contact rule
Apply elite/alpha zombie contact rule
Apply brute zombie contact rule
test_cases.expected_result: Normal, elite/alpha, and brute zombie contact apply 10, 15, and 20 life damage respectively and each successful contact increments civilians lost by exactly 1.

test_cases.title: Overlap targeting stays deterministic across repeated identical frames
preconditions: Target resolver can receive repeated identical entity arrays
test_cases.given: A civilian and a normal zombie overlap under the same crosshair position with identical coordinates and spawn order across 20 repeated evaluations
test_cases.when: The shot target resolver is executed 20 times with the exact same crosshair and entity inputs
test_cases.then: The same target is returned in all 20 evaluations with no variation
steps: Construct one repeated overlap frame with stable input data
Execute target resolution 20 times
Compare all returned target IDs
test_cases.expected_result: When multiple entities overlap under the crosshair, hit resolution returns the same target outcome across repeated identical overlap conditions.

test_cases.title: Baseline exposes exactly three zombie categories
preconditions: Enemy type table or runtime categories are inspectable
test_cases.given: The game baseline enemy definitions are loaded
test_cases.when: The enemy categories are enumerated for active gameplay
test_cases.then: Exactly three enemy categories exist: normal zombie, elite/alpha zombie, and brute zombie
steps: Inspect the runtime category definitions
Enumerate all gameplay enemy categories
Confirm the exact category count and names
test_cases.expected_result: The game exposes exactly three enemy categories in the product baseline: normal zombie, elite/alpha zombie, and brute zombie.

test_cases.title: Threat escalates by measurable gameplay factor across levels
preconditions: Level progression data is available
test_cases.given: Level 1 and Level 5 are initialized under the same test harness
test_cases.when: Spawn interval, active threat limit, and enemy pressure values are compared
test_cases.then: At least one measurable factor such as spawn rate, active threat volume, enemy durability, or chase pressure increases between Level 1 and Level 5
steps: Initialize level 1 profile
Initialize level 5 profile
Compare measurable difficulty outputs
test_cases.expected_result: Each level increases threat through at least one measurable factor such as spawn rate, active threat volume, enemy durability, or chase pressure.

test_cases.title: Wave model mixes threat and protection pressure without fixed ratio dependency
preconditions: Wave generation or spawn orchestration can be observed over multiple cycles
test_cases.given: Three consecutive waves are simulated in the same level under deterministic test timing
test_cases.when: Active civilians and threats are sampled across the wave sequence
test_cases.then: Each sampled wave includes both threat pressure and civilian-protection pressure, while the test does not assert a fixed zombie-to-civilian ratio
steps: Start one deterministic level session
Sample three wave windows
Verify presence of both civilian-protection and threat pressure in each wave
test_cases.expected_result: Wave composition includes both threat and protection pressure, but requirements do not depend on a fixed zombie-to-civilian spawn ratio.

test_cases.title: HUD shows score, level, civilians saved/lost, and best-run context
preconditions: An active run is visible at 800x450 and 1280x720
test_cases.given: The game is running at 800x450 and then at 1280x720 with a visible active session
test_cases.when: The HUD is inspected during active gameplay
test_cases.then: Current score, level, civilians saved, civilians lost, and current or best run context are all visible during active gameplay
steps: Start a run at 800x450
Inspect HUD fields
Repeat at 1280x720
Confirm all required values remain visible
test_cases.expected_result: The HUD displays current score, level, civilians saved, civilians lost, and current or best run context during active gameplay.

test_cases.title: Run closure reports score before restart or continuation
preconditions: Game can enter both level-complete and game-over states
test_cases.given: One run ends in level complete at score 220 and another run ends in game over at score 145
test_cases.when: The summary overlays are shown before any restart or continuation action
test_cases.then: Each summary reports the player's achieved score before restart or continuation becomes available
steps: Force one level-complete outcome with score 220
Inspect the level-complete summary
Force one game-over outcome with score 145
Inspect the game-over summary
test_cases.expected_result: A run ends with a clear game-over or level-closure state that reports the player's achieved score before restart or continuation.

test_cases.title: High score survives browser reload through localStorage
preconditions: Browser localStorage is available
A run score higher than the current record can be produced
test_cases.given: localStorage key `zrr.highScore` starts at value `120` and the current run ends with score `185`
test_cases.when: The game persists the score, the browser reloads, and the HUD is rendered again
test_cases.then: localStorage key `zrr.highScore` equals `185` after reload and the best score is displayed as 185 in the next session
steps: Set localStorage `zrr.highScore` to 120
Finish a run with score 185
Reload the page
Read localStorage and HUD best score
test_cases.expected_result: The highest recorded score persists across a browser reload in 100% of standard local runs where localStorage is available.

test_cases.title: Shipped gameplay-critical art contains no placeholder geometry or mixed final-art styles
preconditions: Final gameplay-critical assets are available in the build artifact
test_cases.given: The shipped build contains gameplay-critical character, bunker, crosshair, and HUD assets
test_cases.when: The art set is reviewed against the asset manifest and final build render output
test_cases.then: Gameplay-critical characters, bunker, crosshair, and HUD use a consistent visual style with no placeholder geometry or mixed final-art styles in the shipped build
steps: Open the shipped build
Inspect gameplay-critical assets on screen
Cross-check them against the asset inventory
test_cases.expected_result: Gameplay-critical characters, bunker, crosshair, and HUD use a consistent visual style with no placeholder geometry or mixed final-art styles in the shipped build.

test_cases.title: Major action classes produce distinct visual and audio feedback within timing threshold
preconditions: Audio context has been unlocked by player interaction
test_cases.given: An active session can trigger shooting, zombie hit, zombie death, civilian damage, level complete, and game over events
test_cases.when: Each event is triggered once in an instrumented test session
test_cases.then: Each major action class has a distinct feedback signal and audio playback starts within 100ms of the trigger event on an unlocked audio context
steps: Unlock audio with first interaction
Trigger each major action class event
Measure feedback timing and compare signal distinctness
test_cases.expected_result: Every major action class — shooting, zombie hit, zombie death, civilian damage, level complete, and game over — has a distinct feedback signal, with audio playback starting within 100ms of the trigger event on an unlocked audio context.

test_cases.title: HUD remains legible at 800x450 and 1280x720 without overlap
preconditions: HUD is visible in active gameplay
test_cases.given: The game is rendered once at 800x450 and once at 1280x720
test_cases.when: HUD text and overlays are inspected while score, level, civilians saved, civilians lost, and accuracy are all non-zero
test_cases.then: HUD text remains legible at 800x450 and 1280x720 with no overlap that obscures score, level, civilians saved, civilians lost, or accuracy
steps: Render active gameplay at 800x450 with populated HUD values
Inspect overlap and readability
Render active gameplay at 1280x720 with populated HUD values
Inspect overlap and readability
test_cases.expected_result: HUD text remains legible at 800x450 and 1280x720 with no overlap that obscures score, level, civilians saved, civilians lost, or accuracy.

test_cases.title: Gameplay-critical assets are fully represented in inventory and ASSETS documentation
preconditions: Boot asset loader paths are available
Asset manifest and ASSETS.md exist
test_cases.given: The shipped runtime loads gameplay-critical visual and audio assets from local paths and the repository contains `public/assets/manifest.json` plus `ASSETS.md`
test_cases.when: All gameplay-critical loaded asset paths are compared against the asset inventory and ASSETS documentation
test_cases.then: 100% of gameplay-critical visual and audio assets loaded by the shipped runtime are listed in the project asset inventory and documented in ASSETS.md
steps: Enumerate gameplay-critical loaded assets from the runtime loader
Cross-check each asset against `public/assets/manifest.json`
Cross-check each asset against `ASSETS.md`
test_cases.expected_result: 100% of gameplay-critical visual and audio assets loaded by the shipped runtime are listed in the project asset inventory and documented in ASSETS.md.

test_cases.title: Runtime rejects non-local executable dependency paths
preconditions: Security policy helper can evaluate candidate asset paths
test_cases.given: Candidate asset paths include `https://cdn.example.com/zombie.js`, `javascript:alert(1)`, `/assets/sprites/zombie-pixel.svg`, and `/assets/../secrets.txt`
test_cases.when: The security policy evaluates these paths for runtime safety
test_cases.then: The browser runtime accepts only the local-safe asset path and rejects non-local or unsafe executable paths
steps: Evaluate the four candidate asset paths through the security helper
Collect accepted and rejected results
Assert that only the safe local asset path is accepted
test_cases.expected_result: The browser runtime performs no gameplay-critical remote script execution and no third-party executable asset loading during a standard session.

test_cases.title: Normal play exposes no admin or debug route to end users
preconditions: Game is running in standard local or embedded mode
test_cases.given: The game is running in standard local play mode and the player has access only to the shipped browser surface
test_cases.when: The browser routes, visible controls, and standard runtime interactions are inspected during normal play
test_cases.then: The embedded or local runtime exposes no admin or debug route to an end user during normal play
steps: Start the game in local or embedded mode
Inspect visible controls and routes
Review normal runtime interactions for admin/debug entry points
test_cases.expected_result: The embedded or local runtime exposes no admin or debug route to an end user during normal play.
